reasons: Tigreal can group enemies into Vale's burst area.
Vale can follow Tigreal's engage from behind the front line.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the crowd-control start, while Vale adds AoE magic burst into the grouped target area.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Vale is in range.
Vale saves his burst until Tigreal confirms the catch.
failureCases: Vale casts before enemies are grouped.
Enemy assassins flank Vale during the combo.

reasons: Atlas can pull enemies into a compact group for Vale's AoE burst.
Vale can punish the displacement window without needing to start the fight himself.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies, while Vale follows with burst damage and control into the same area.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches multiple enemies near an objective or choke.
Vale holds ultimate until the pull is confirmed.
failureCases: Vale is too far behind to follow.
Enemy spreads before Atlas reaches them.

reasons: Minotaur provides frontline control that gives Vale a safer damage window.
Vale can layer magic burst after Minotaur disrupts enemy positioning.

proof.summary: Minotaur starts or absorbs the fight, while Vale adds ranged magic damage after enemies are slowed or knocked up.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur controls the front line before Vale commits.
Vale positions behind Minotaur during objective fights.
failureCases: Minotaur engages before Vale is nearby.
Vale gets flanked while focusing on the front line.
Enemy disengages after Minotaur uses key control.

reasons: Kaja can suppress one target and bring it into Vale's burst range.
Vale provides the damage follow-up that Kaja needs after forcing the pick.

proof.summary: Kaja locks and drags a priority target, while Vale delivers burst during the guaranteed control window.
worksBestWhen: Vale saves burst until Kaja confirms suppression.
failureCases: Kaja pulls the target outside Vale's skill angle.
Vale lacks vision or gets zoned before the follow-up.
The enemy target survives and counters after the burst.

reasons: Carmilla's linked ultimate can spread part of Vale's damage and control value across multiple enemies.
Vale's AoE burst becomes more dangerous when enemies are linked and grouped.

proof.summary: Carmilla links enemy heroes, while Vale uses burst and control that can pressure the linked group.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla lands Curse of Blood on multiple enemies.
Vale waits for the link before spending his main burst.
failureCases: Vale bursts before the link is active.
Enemies split apart or disengage from the linked area.
